lesson.menu_title: Presenting to Leadership -- The Elevator Pitch
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Presenting to Leadership -- The Elevator Pitch
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 8 -- Presenting to Leadership

listenings.text: Hi Natalie, this is David Reed, VP of Sales. I am calling about the quarterly leadership review on Thursday. I would like you to present the public sector results. You will have two minutes, so keep it short and clear. Please open with your value proposition, then highlight the impact on the business with a few strong numbers. The directors want one clear takeaway, not a long report. You have done great work this quarter, so tell that story with confidence. Remember to tailor the pitch for the leadership team. Send me your one-pager before Thursday. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hi, this is Natalie, rehearsing my pitch for the leadership review. Here it is. Good morning, everyone. Our value proposition is simple: faster, safer cloud services for the public sector. This quarter we have closed a lot of deals and signed several new clients. We have delivered plenty of value, and our biggest project has reduced costs for the client. The outcome is a three-year contract with the Ministry. I have one clear takeaway for you today: our public sector pipeline has grown, and it has doubled this year. Thank you. I am happy to answer your questions.

extra_audio.text: First, you tailor the pitch for your audience. Then you open with your value proposition in one line. After that, you highlight the impact with a few strong numbers. Next, you share the outcome you have achieved. Finally, you end with one clear takeaway to persuade them.
